item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OMG, I love this, I want more experiment log with this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like this and enjoy some of the addenda logs, but I have a gripe with the containment procedures in particilar. In-universe, the "Shark Punching Center" would never refer to themselves as such. It's either the Centre or Center, similar to the SCP Foundation simply calling themselves the Foundation. As well, SPC itself is from an alternate universe, so I don't understand their inclusion in this scip at all except for some offhand humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For a second I thought it was a SPC article with the shark picture. But yea I really enjoyed this!

+1

item.title: cool beans
item.content:encoded:
i'd pay all the money in the world to have a shark that rambles about random shit all day.

+1 for making me exhale slightly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't you hate it when you're swimming and then a shark comes by and annoys you with a 1-minute unskippable ad? Worry not, subscribe to the SCP-4499's [REDACTED] only for $44.99 per month to skip ads!

Yea that'd be funny.

I actually thought there is only one instance of SCP-4499 but i just realized some of them dialogue between themselves in the last part of document. I really like the idea, and the fact that it even spills out the truth of SCP-001 makes it interesting.

Putting EEG scan machine on sharks must be difficult.

+1

item.title: Quick comment
item.content:encoded: So, this is just SCP-000-J really. Not that it's bad but still

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem with that version of the rule of threes is that a lot of otherwise harmless SCPs have this issue where the anomaly is otherwise completely harmless and fun, but out of the blue there's an incident where it suddenly either kills a bunch of people or shows that it knows classified information. This can be done well, don't get me wrong (look at 2317 and 1230), but when the "danger" is just an offhand remark and the article doesn't really delve into the effects it has on the foundation/anomaly, it just kinda falls flat.

About the second issue - I think it'd be just fine, really. Maybe make up some anomalies if you feel like it's too much of a crosslinker, but it's a short-ish article so it shouldn't be an issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback!
The 001 pitch was an attempt to play with the rule of threes, and kinda give the skip a bit more depth than just feeling like it should've been a -J article. I also didn't want to feel like I'd just crammed as many references in as possible, and I almost felt like I'd done too many as-is: including 173 there are six, adding more feels like I'm basically making a glorified summary of various other SCPs without doing anything unique. That's also why I ended up keeping the last conversation short - trying more pitches means either throwing in more references, or other made-up anomalies, which I felt would end up being the same punchline and come off as tacky. I'll see if I can come up with a solution on your concern though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the concept, but I feel like it could have been more.

The 001 pitch kinda comes outta nowhere and doesn't add anything - if anything, it detracts from the humor - and I think replacing it with another pitch would make it overall better. Also, for me atleast it felt as if the conversation the sharks had was very short (Maybe had the shark tried another pitch that didn't work it would've helped?).

I really wanna upvote, but there isn't enough stuff there. Nonvoting

item.title: Credit where it's due
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to everyone who reviewed this draft! Special thanks to:
amnestic_protocol for reviewing this draft more times than any man should.
T_Kirsansky for helping me convert this from a -J to a proper mainlist.
And everyone in #TheCritters who gave feedback too!

Image credit: Great White by Elias Levy, licensed under Creative Commons, available at This Flickr
